test_suite.details: node test.js: 3403 passed, 0 failed across all 8 sections (wins.json schema, HTML consistency, internal links, build pipeline, cross-references, sections.json schema, prose integrity, prediction panels including 78 prediction panels: 13 tombstone, 65 mined, 10 operational).

internal_anchors.details: Covered by test.js Section 3 (Internal Links) — all href=#X targets resolve, all showTab() calls reference real tabs. Not re-checked per integrity-agent spec (don't duplicate the test suite).

tab_structure.details: Covered by test.js Section 2 (HTML Output Consistency) and Section 3 (Internal Links).

timeouts.detail: SSL handshake timeout on two attempts (12s HEAD and 60s GET). Same behavior observed in 2026-04-21 report — persistent PO.DAAC infrastructure issue.
SSL handshake timeout, same root cause (shared PO.DAAC infra).

ua_sensitive.detail: Returns 403 to curl with default UA; returns 200 with a Safari-like browser UA. Not broken — bot-blocking.
Same UA-sensitivity as noaa.gov root; returns 200 with browser UA.

external_links.details: 34 unique data-source URLs checked (non-citation, non-GitHub, non-dome-site, non-wikipedia). 30 returned 200. 2 NOAA URLs were UA-blocked to curl but returned 200 to a realistic browser UA — flag as ok. 2 podaac.jpl.nasa.gov URLs timed out at TLS handshake (persistent PO.DAAC infra issue, flagged yesterday). DOI/publisher links skipped (Curmudgeon's scope).

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 71 entries (69 numeric IDs 001-069 + 057b + 058b). docs/index.html contains 71 id="winNNN" anchors and the Verdict Summary Table has 71 tbody rows — one-to-one match. Verdict tally: Refuted by Data 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sums to 71). new_in_v51 count: 32. FUSE data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html md5sums are identical to git HEAD.

win_detail_consistency.details: Every wins.json entry has a matching id="winNNN" anchor. All 71 entries expose tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Summary table has 71 tbody rows with proper verdict class names (v-refuted, v-selfcon, v-misleading, v-std, v-notdem, v-unfals). Note: no asterisk marker rendered for new_in_v51 in the summary table — this is a template design choice (generate-html.js does not emit one) and is consistent with 2026-04-21's pass on this check.

win_dome_alignment.details: Fetched https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html (91,686 bytes). Dome site exposes WIN-001..WIN-069 (69 contiguous numbers) plus PROS-001..PROS-008 aliases. Our wins.json covers 69 base numeric IDs plus 057b/058b. Sample title spot-checks across WIN-001, 010, 020, 030, 040, 050, 060, 067, 068, 069 show no title drift between dome and our claims. No number collisions requiring investigation.

progressive_disclosure.details: 116 ps-summary and 116 ps-tldr occurrences (prose sections). 227 ks-summary and 227 ks-tldr occurrences (kill-shot/WIN panels). 142 win-section details blocks (71 WINs x 2 sections = Evidence + Verdict). All 71 wins.json entries carry tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict. Spot-check rendering matches expected structure.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: 2 hardcoded light colors in data/sections.json — unchanged from 2026-04-21: (1) part1.html inline style 'background: #f5f5f5; ... border-left: 3px solid #666' around the g(r,φ) formula block; (2) part6.html inline style 'background:#fff' on the US787412A patent thumbnail wrapper. Both render as bright panels against the dark theme. Fix by replacing with var(--card-bg) / var(--accent).

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt present (5493 bytes), contains 'funwithscience.net/dome-model-review' URL and mentions the dome model by name; references '69 dome claims (V51.1)' which matches dome site's 69 contiguous numeric WINs. docs/sitemap.xml valid XML with <urlset>, 2 <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt) — both URLs serve. docs/robots.txt has 'Allow: /' and 'Sitemap: https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml'. <head> has <meta name="description">, <meta property="og:title">, <meta property="og:description">, and <script type="application/ld+json"> with @type ClaimReview. data/uncounted-failures.json has 10 FAIL-NNN entries (FAIL-001..FAIL-010), zero duplicates, zero missing required fields; HTML og:description references '10 acknowledged failures' — consistent.

expansion_tracker.id_gap: EXP-052 missing (historical — no work lost, carried from prior runs)
expansion_tracker.tracker_orphan_new: EXP-217.json exists on workspace but no matching tracker entry — see workspace_only_files finding
expansion_tracker.details: next_id=217 > max_id=216 — Phase 0 invariant satisfied, no imminent ID collision. 0 phantom entries (all complete tracker items with output_file resolve). 0 stale pending (>48h) items. One historical gap (EXP-052) persists from earlier reports. 175 files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ are not referenced from the tracker — 83 EXP-*, 81 PRED-batch-*, 8 ISS-*, 2 MODE5, 1 PROS; these are archaeological (FUSE cannot unlink) or analyst-writes-without-tracker-link patterns seen for EXP-178/191/192 etc. The one that matters: EXP-217.json (mtime 07:02 UTC) has no tracker entry at all (unlike EXP-178/191/192 which have tracker entries with output_file:undefined). This is likely a mid-workflow write; tracker was last modified 07:08 after the file was written, suggesting the tracker update step was skipped or failed. Not yet a collision risk but worth reconciling — see issues_found.

workspace_only_files.details: Two completed-work files exist on FUSE workspace but not in git. (1) WIN-010.c5.json — curmudgeon cycle-5 review of WIN-010, written 2026-04-22T06:39Z (~1h before this run). (2) EXP-217.json — analyst's ISS-1281 (PRED-105 cucumber-growth silent-ignore) resolution findings, status ready-for-integration, written 2026-04-22T07:02Z (~25m before this run). Both should be picked up by workspace-sync within 4h. EXP-217 additionally lacks a tracker entry (see expansion_tracker); if workspace-sync commits the file but the tracker never gets an entry, subsequent next_id allocation could reuse 217.

section_new_queue_coupling.details: Zero expansion-tracker items with category section-new or category-proposal-writeup in status != integrated. Nothing currently requires a curmudgeon re-review gate.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 69 skip log entries in last 24h — all mtime-guard (normal: git copy is newer than workspace). Zero direction-violation skips. Top paths: CAT-005.json, EXP-190-proposal.json, PRED-batch-2026-04-18.json, WIN-020.c4.json, WIN-058.c5.json, daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json (6 each), WIN-012.c5, WIN-053.c7, WIN-064.c4, WIN-068.c8 (5 each). No boundary-crossing bugs detected.

curmudgeon_digest_coverage.details: Fresh digest regeneration (build-scripts/digest-reviews.js) exit 0. 359 review files → 215 unique base IDs, 345 processed, 12 unprocessed. 12 exceeds the 10-unprocessed major threshold on paper, but all 12 have mtimes within the last 24h (earliest: 2026-04-21T14:33Z for EXP-202-proposal.json; latest: 2026-04-22T06:39Z for WIN-010.c5.json), and prefixes are mixed (EXP:3, SEC:1, WIN:8) — not a single-prefix systematic filter bug. This is pipeline lag (decider hasn't run since curmudgeon's latest outputs), not a regression in digest-reviews.js. Flag as warn rather than major. Unprocessed files: EXP-202-proposal.json, EXP-208-proposal.json, EXP-210-proposal.json, SEC-8b.3.c4.json, WIN-004.c3.json, WIN-010.c5.json, WIN-012.c6.json, WIN-014.c3.json, WIN-031.c4.json, WIN-038.c7.json, WIN-064.c5.json, WIN-067.c7.json.

broken_review_files.details: Freshly regenerated digest reports 0 parse errors. No curmudgeon reviews are in error limbo.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned fresh from origin/main at /tmp/dome-integrity-clone (HEAD 69215d7 'Decider E2: queue pop, pop WIN-038.c7 claim, shadow log'), copied workspace node_modules, ran 'node build.js html'. git diff HEAD -- docs/index.html returns 0 bytes — published docs/index.html exactly matches what wins.json + sections.json + generate-html.js produce. FUSE data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html md5s match git clone md5s.

project_documentation.details: CLAUDE.md schedule table lists 8 agents; monitor/prompts/ contains 9 .md files (8 agent prompts + prose-extraction-task.md — not an agent). All 8 referenced agent prompt files exist. Spot-checked 17 key file paths (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, data/predictions.json, build.js, test.js, docs/index.html, docs/llms.txt, docs/sitemap.xml, docs/robots.txt, monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json, monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, and all 4 prompt files) — all exist. No hardcoded count numbers detected in CLAUDE.md (no 'N WIN', 'N issue', 'N test passed' patterns).

issues_found.description: Completed curmudgeon cycle-5 review WIN-010.c5.json exists on FUSE workspace but is not committed to git. Will be lost on workspace recycle until workspace-sync runs.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Wait for next workspace-sync cycle (≤ 4h) or manually cp to a clean clone and commit.

issues_found.description: Analyst output EXP-217.json (ISS-1281 PRED-105 cucumber-growth silent-ignore resolution, status ready-for-integration) exists on FUSE workspace but is not committed to git, AND has no corresponding entry in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (tracker.next_id=217, tracker.items has no id=EXP-217). Tracker was last modified at 07:08Z after the file was written at 07:02Z, so the tracker update step appears to have been skipped or failed in the analyst run. This makes EXP-217 invisible to the tracker-based orphan/phantom checks and could permit 217 to be reallocated to a different item on the next analyst run (same risk pattern that caused EXP-211 collision earlier today).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider: when integrating EXP-217 (ISS-1281 resolution), append a tracker item with id=EXP-217, status=integrated, output_file=monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-217.json, and bump tracker.next_id to 218 in the same write. Alternatively, the analyst prompt should be tightened so that tracker append + file write happen atomically in the same commit.

issues_found.description: 12 curmudgeon reviews unprocessed by decider, slightly over the 10-item major threshold. All 12 have mtimes within the last 24h and prefixes are mixed (EXP:3, SEC:1, WIN:8) — consistent with pipeline lag rather than systematic filter bug. Downgrade to moderate: no evidence of regression in digest-reviews.js; decider just hasn't run since curmudgeon's latest outputs landed.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Let the next decider run chew through the pending items. If still >10 after next decider run, re-examine for filter regression.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded light background '#f5f5f5' in an inline style around the g(r,φ) formula block in part1.html — renders as a bright panel against the dark theme. Unchanged from 2026-04-21 report.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'background: #f5f5f5' with 'background: var(--card-bg)' and 'border-left: 3px solid #666' with 'border-left: 3px solid var(--accent)'.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded light background '#fff' on the US787412A patent thumbnail wrapper in part6.html — renders as a bright white rectangle against the dark theme. Unchanged from 2026-04-21 report.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'background:#fff' with 'background: var(--card-bg)' or remove the inline background entirely.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 remains missing from expansion-tracker.json (items jump from EXP-051 to EXP-053) and no EXP-052.json exists. next_id invariant still holds (next_id=217 > max_id=216). This is a historical anomaly carried from prior reports — no new drift. Most likely an aborted allocation, not lost work.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Search git history for 'EXP-052' to confirm it was never allocated. If confirmed, document as intentionally-skipped in a tracker note; otherwise recover from history.

issues_found.description: https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ and https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/.../ProdSpecDoc_v4.6.pdf both time out at TLS handshake on multiple attempts (12s HEAD, 30s+ GET). Persistent across 2026-04-21 and 2026-04-22 runs — this is PO.DAAC infrastructure flakiness, not a broken link.
issues_found.suggested_fix: If the timeout persists for another 2-3 consecutive runs, consider replacing with the specific dataset landing page or a NASA Earthdata alternative.

issues_found.description: https://www.noaa.gov/ and https://www.noaa.gov/jetstream/global/global-atmospheric-circulations return 403 to curl with default UA, but 200 to a realistic browser UA (Safari/Chrome). Not broken — bot-blocking. Informational only.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action needed. The integrity agent's URL checker already retries with a browser UA; this is noted so future runs don't misclassify as broken.

summary: Overall warn. Core site health remains solid: test suite 3403/3403, build fully reproducible (FUSE data and docs match git HEAD, clone+build diff = 0), all 71 WIN anchors and tbody rows resolve, 69 dome WIN numbers align, tab chain complete, discoverability intact, 0 parse errors, 0 direction-violation skips, 0 phantom tracker entries. Two major findings are workspace-only files at risk: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-010.c5.json (fresh today ~1h ago) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-217.json (fresh ~25m ago). The EXP-217 case is the more serious of the two — not only is the file uncommitted, but the tracker has no entry for EXP-217 at all despite being written after the file. This reproduces the same non-atomic allocation pattern that caused the EXP-211 collision at 00:09Z today; 217 could be reallocated if another analyst run claims it before the tracker is updated. Moderate: 12 unprocessed curmudgeon reviews (1 over the major threshold but all <24h old, mixed prefixes — downgraded to warn/moderate; likely just pipeline lag awaiting next decider run), two unchanged hardcoded light theme colors in sections.json (part1 g(r,φ) block, part6 patent thumbnail), and the longstanding EXP-052 tracker gap. Minor: PO.DAAC SSL-handshake timeouts (persistent infrastructure issue, not our problem), NOAA UA-blocking (not broken).
